sections.heading: Overview: Cal/OSHA and the California State Plan

content.text: California operates its own OSHA program — known as Cal/OSHA — under an OSHA-approved State Plan. This means that Cal/OSHA standards apply to Contoso Retail's California warehouses instead of (and often in addition to) federal OSHA standards. Importantly, Cal/OSHA regulations are frequently more stringent than federal OSHA requirements.

The Division of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH), commonly called Cal/OSHA, is the enforcement arm. DOSH conducts inspections, investigates complaints and incidents, and issues citations and penalties for violations.

sections.heading: Key Cal/OSHA Regulations for Warehouse Operations

rows: Every California employer must have a written IIPP. Cal/OSHA can inspect the IIPP at any time.
Mandatory written plan, shade required at 80°F, cool-down rest periods. See CA Heat Illness Prevention document.
Operator training and certification required; stricter maintenance recordkeeping than federal.
Written EAP required for all facilities with 10+ employees.
Mirrors federal HazCom but includes Proposition 65 additional warning requirements.
Cal/OSHA requires an ergonomics program for repetitive motion injuries affecting 2+ workers in the same job.
Employer must conduct PPE hazard assessment and certify it in writing.

content.text: California's IIPP requirement (8 CCR §3203) is broader and more detailed than federal OSHA's equivalent. Every Contoso Retail California facility must maintain a written IIPP that includes:

items: A responsible person or persons with authority and responsibility for the IIPP.
A system for ensuring employee compliance with safe and healthy work practices.
A system for communicating with employees about health and safety matters — including anonymous reporting.
Procedures for identifying and evaluating workplace hazards.
Procedures for investigating occupational injuries and illnesses.
Methods for correcting unsafe or unhealthy conditions in a timely manner.
Training and instruction provisions for all employees, including supervisors.

content.text: The Warehouse Manager is the designated IIPP Program Administrator for each California facility. The IIPP is reviewed and updated annually and after any serious incident.
California reporting timelines are stricter than federal OSHA:

Report Cal/OSHA emergencies to: Cal/OSHA 24-hour emergency line: 1-800-963-9424. The Safety Coordinator must also notify the Warehouse Manager and Regional Director immediately for any reportable incident.
California workers have all federal OSHA rights plus additional California-specific protections:

items: Right to request a Cal/OSHA inspection anonymously without employer notification.
Right to file a complaint with the Labor Commissioner if retaliated against for safety activity.
Right to refuse work that poses an imminent danger of serious injury or death, with specific Cal/OSHA-defined protections for that refusal.
Right to access the facility's IIPP, injury/illness logs, and exposure records.
California Labor Code §6310 provides additional anti-retaliation protections beyond federal OSHA.

content.text: California's Repetitive Motion Injury (RMI) standard (8 CCR §5110) requires Contoso Retail to implement an ergonomics program when a repetitive motion injury has been diagnosed by a licensed physician in two or more workers in the same job type within a 12-month period.

items: The program must include a worksite evaluation to identify and correct risk factors.
Control measures must be implemented (rotation, mechanical assists, workstation redesign).
Affected employees must receive training on ergonomic risk factors and injury recognition.
The Safety Coordinator tracks RMI diagnoses and triggers a program review if the threshold is met.
